summary:
The latent phase of labor begins before the active phase, usually at around 3-4 cm dilatation, and does not start at its conclusion. Statement B falsely claims it begins at the end of the active phase, making this claim inaccurate. 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{B}}$

The latent phase of labor initiates prior to the active phase, generally beginning at 3–4 cm cervical dilation and progressing up to 6–8 cm. Claiming it starts at the end of the active phase (option B) is clinically erroneous because phases follow a defined sequence from latent to active.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{B}}$

The latent phase of labor initiates prior to the active phase, generally beginning at 3–4 cm cervical dilation and progressing up to 6–8 cm. Claiming it starts at the end of the active phase (option B) is clinically erroneous because phases follow a defined sequence from latent to active.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{B}}$. Answer choice: B
